title: NOC 21310 – Electrical and Electronics Engineers | GTR Immigration
description: Electrical and Electronics Engineers are in strong demand across Canada, driven by infrastructure expansion, clean-energy projects, telecommunications upgrades, automation, and advanced manufacturing. Classified under NOC 21310 (TEER 1), this occupation is highly suitable for Express Entry, Provincial Nominee Programs (PNPs), LMIA-supported work permits, and long-term Permanent Residence (PR) strategies.

classification.category: 2 – Natural and applied sciences and related occupations
classification.teer: 1 – Occupations usually require a university degree

duties: Design and analyze power-generation and distribution networks
Develop electrical circuits, components, and control systems
Prepare project specifications and cost estimates
employment_requirements: Bachelor’s degree in electrical or electronics engineering
Master’s/PhD for advanced or research roles (sometimes)
Completion of supervised Canadian work experience
job_outlook: Electrical & Electronics Engineering remains immigration-relevant, especially for professionals experienced in:
additional_information: Canada’s utilities, transportation systems, telecom networks, R&D labs, and electronics manufacturers continue to recruit Electrical & Electronics Engineers for:
Power-generation & distribution projectsCommunications networksAutomation & instrumentation systemsTransportation infrastructureSemiconductor & electronics development
Typical annual salaries range between $55,600–$133,200 CAD, depending on province, specialization, and licensing status.
